name: GT-ucp-positive-square
source: Kitaev arXiv:2405.02434, refs/kitaev-2405.02434/approximate_algebras.tex:1691-1717 (UCP inequalities), VERBATIM: "The following inequality holds for any UCP map $\Phi\colon\calA\to\calB$ between $C^*$ algebras:" "\Phi(X^\dag)\,\Phi(X)\le \Phi(X^\dag X)." and "Another general inequality is $\|\Phi(X)\|\le\|X\|$. It follows from \eqref{PhiXdX} and the positivity of $\Phi$, which implies that $\Phi(X^\dag X)\le \Phi(\|X\|^2I_\calA) =\|X\|^2I_\calB$." We use the POSITIVITY of the UCP map F (=Kitaev Phi): F is completely positive hence positive (order-preserving on self-adjoints). For self-adjoint X, X^2=X^dag X >= 0 in B(H), so F(X^2) >= 0; and F(X^2)=F(X^dag X) <= ||X||^2 1. (NOTE: Ftilde itself is NOT guaranteed completely positive --- :363 --- so JB3 must route through F, not Ftilde.)
